chunks.text: Stereo. HC JD A 38. Judgment Sheet IN THE LAHORE HIGH COURT, BAHAWALPUR BENCH, BAHAWALPUR. JUDICIAL DEPARTMENT Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 (The State Vs. 1. Wazeer 2. Muhammad Ali) Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 (Wazeer and another Vs. The State and another) Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. (Muhammad Asghar Vs. The State and two others.) J U D G M E N T Date of hearing: 15.02.2022. Appellants by: Mr. Muhammad Sharif Bhatti, Advocate. State by: Ch. Asghar Ali Gill, Deputy Prosecutor General. Complainant by: Syed Aasim Ali Bukhari, Advocate. SADIQ MAHMUD KHURRAM, J. –Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad (convicts) were tried along with Jafar son of Mukhtiar Ahmad and Sabir Hussain son of Rab Nawaz ( the co-accused of the convicts, both since acquitted) by the learned Additional Sessions Judge/ Model Criminal Trial Court, Khanpur in the case instituted upon the private complaint titled “Muhammad Asghar Vs. Wazir and three others” (relating to FIR No. 49 of 2017 dated 06.02.2017 registered at Police Station Saddar Khanpur District Rahim Yar Khan) in respect of offences under sections 302, Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 2 311, 148 and 149 PPC for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Muhammad Ehsan son of Muhammad Asghar (deceased) and Tahira Bibi daughter of Ibrahim (deceased). The learned trial court vide judgment dated 19.07.2019, convicted Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad (convicts) and sentenced them as infra: Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad: Death on two counts under section 302(b) PPC as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Tahira Bibi daughter of Ibrahim (deceased) and Muhammad Ehsan son of Muhammad Asghar (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.100,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of both the deceased namely Muhammad Ehsan son of Muhammad Asghar (deceased) and Tahira Bibi daughter of Ibrahim; in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to further undergo six months of simple imprisonment for each default.

Wazir and three others” (relating to FIR No. 49 of 2017 dated 06.02.2017 registered at Police Station Saddar Khanpur District Rahim Yar Khan) in respect of offences under sections 302, Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 2 311, 148 and 149 PPC for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Muhammad Ehsan son of Muhammad Asghar (deceased) and Tahira Bibi daughter of Ibrahim (deceased). The learned trial court vide judgment dated 19.07.2019, convicted Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad (convicts) and sentenced them as infra: Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad: Death on two counts under section 302(b) PPC as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Tahira Bibi daughter of Ibrahim (deceased) and Muhammad Ehsan son of Muhammad Asghar (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.100,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of both the deceased namely Muhammad Ehsan son of Muhammad Asghar (deceased) and Tahira Bibi daughter of Ibrahim; in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to further undergo six months of simple imprisonment for each default. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad: i) Death under section 302(b) PPC as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Muhammad Ehsan son of Muhammad Asghar (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.100,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased namely Muhammad Ehsan son of Muhammad Asghar (deceased), in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to further undergo six months of simple imprisonment. The convict was ordered to be hanged by his neck till dead. ii) Imprisonment for life under section 302(b) PPC as Tazir for committing Qatl-i-Amd of Tahira Bibi daughter of Ibrahim (deceased) and directed to pay Rs.100,000/- as compensation under section 544-A, Cr.P.C. to the legal heirs of the deceased namely Tahira Bibi daughter of Ibrahim; in case of default thereof, the convict was directed to further undergo six months of simple imprisonment. The convict namely Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad was extended the benefit available under Section 382-B of Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 by the learned trial court. Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 3 Jafar son of Mukhtiar Ahmad and Sabir Hussain son of Rab Nawaz, the co-accused of the convicts, were acquitted by the learned trial court. 2. Feeling aggrieved, Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad (convicts) lodged the Criminal Appeal No.489 of 2019 assailing their convictions and sentences. The learned trial court submitted Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 under section 374 Cr.P.C. seeking confirmation or otherwise of the sentences of death awarded to the appellants namely Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad.

Feeling aggrieved, Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad (convicts) lodged the Criminal Appeal No.489 of 2019 assailing their convictions and sentences. The learned trial court submitted Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 under section 374 Cr.P.C. seeking confirmation or otherwise of the sentences of death awarded to the appellants namely Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad. The complainant of the case namely Muhammad Asghar filed Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019 seeking permission to file an appeal against the acquittal of Jafar son of Mukhtiar Ahmad and Sabir Hussain son of Rab Nawaz (the co-accused of the convicts, both since acquitted). We intend to dispose of the Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019, Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 and Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019 through this single judgment. 3. Precisely, the necessary facts of the prosecution case, as stated by Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), the complainant of the case, are as under:- “I am farmer and I owned 5/6 Tractor Trolleys and used those Tractor Trolleys for supply of sand and soil to brick-kilns, for that purpose I engaged labour and my son Ehsan (deceased) used to check the labour above mentioned and oftenly go to TIBBA Chak. No.10/P to check the labour. Accused Wazeer present before this Court had suspicion of illicit relations with his wife Mst.Tahira deceased with my son Ehsan. Sometime prior to 06.2.2017, Wazeer accused in the presence of Jalil Ahmad and Muhammad Naeem PWs, informed me that my son Ehsan deceased had illicit relations with his wife, if my son Ehsan did not give up that activity, he would suffer the consequences. On 06.2.2017, my son Ehsan deceased while riding on motorcycle bearing registration No. RNQ/403 went to Chak No.10/P to check the labour, I along with Jalil Ahmad and Muhammad Naeem PW followed him on motorcycle. At about MAGHRIB time, when we reached near damaged Pulli Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 4 (bridge), we saw that Mst.Tahira Bibi deceased was standing on the road, who indicated Ehsan deceased to stop.

RNQ/403 went to Chak No.10/P to check the labour, I along with Jalil Ahmad and Muhammad Naeem PW followed him on motorcycle. At about MAGHRIB time, when we reached near damaged Pulli Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 4 (bridge), we saw that Mst.Tahira Bibi deceased was standing on the road, who indicated Ehsan deceased to stop. As soon as my son Ehsan stopped, meanwhile, Muhammad Ali armed with pistol, Wazeer armed with TOKA, Jafar, Sabir accused present before Court and two unknown person armed with TOKAS, emerged from nearby/adjacent sugarcane crop with their common object. Muhammad Ali accused fired at Ehsan my son with his pistol which hit on his chest and he fell down in injured condition. Meanwhile, Wazeer accused inflicted TOKA blows on the person of Ehsan. Soon after Wazeer accused inflicted TOKA blows on the neck of his wife Tahira Bibi. Sabir, Jafar and two unknown accused persons inflicted TOKA blows on the back side of head of Ehsan deceased and on the left side of head of Tahira Bibi deceased. Accused persons threatened us if we come forward, we would face the same consequences. On hue and cry, people of the locality started to attracted (sic) there while seeing them accused persons while brandishing their respective weapons, fled away in the nearby sugarcane crop, who could not be traced out inspite of search. Police reached at the spot. I submitted application Ex.PA to the Thanedar for registration of case which bears my signature. IO visited the place of occurrence, inspected the place of occurrence, took the dead bodies into his possession for postmortem examination. IO secured blood stained soil from the of respective place of occurrence, took into his possession empty of crime weapon (pistol), Ear Rings of deceased Tahira Bibi, Shoes of deceased persons, Motorcycle registration No.403/RNQ of Ehsan deceased. IO did not investigate my case impartially and he in connivance with accused persons Sabir and Jafar found them not involved in this case.

IO secured blood stained soil from the of respective place of occurrence, took into his possession empty of crime weapon (pistol), Ear Rings of deceased Tahira Bibi, Shoes of deceased persons, Motorcycle registration No.403/RNQ of Ehsan deceased. IO did not investigate my case impartially and he in connivance with accused persons Sabir and Jafar found them not involved in this case. To the extent of Sabir and Jafar, the investigation by the IO is not transparent. I had left no other option but to file this private complaint Ex.PB which bears my signature. I and PWs with our own eyes witnessed Jafar and Sabir while inflicting TOKA blows on the person of Tahira Bibi and Ehsan. Accused persons are real culprits, they be punished as per law.” 4. The accused were summoned to face trial in the case instituted upon the private complaint titled “Muhammad Asghar Vs. Wazir and three others” (relating to FIR No. 49 of 2017 dated 06.02.2017 registered at Police Station Saddar Khanpur District Rahim Yar Khan) in respect of offences under sections 302, 311, 148 and 149 PPC for committing the Qatl-i-Amd of Muhammad Ehsan son of Muhammad Asghar (deceased) and Tahira Bibi daughter of Ibrahim (deceased). The learned trial court framed the charge against the Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 5 accused on 18.12.2017, to which the accused pleaded not guilty and claimed trial. 5. The complainant of the case in order to prove its case got recorded statements of as many as twelve witnesses. The ocular account of the case was furnished by Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6). Muhammad Tariq, ASI (PW-3) stated that on 06.02.2017, he recorded the formal F.I.R (Exh.PA/1). Sami Ullah 2000/C (PW-4) stated that on 06.02.2017 the Investigating Officer of the case handed over to him the dead bodies of the deceased for escorting them to the hospital and he also received the last worn clothes of both the deceased from the Medical Officers after the post mortem examinations of the dead bodies of the deceased.

Muhammad Tariq, ASI (PW-3) stated that on 06.02.2017, he recorded the formal F.I.R (Exh.PA/1). Sami Ullah 2000/C (PW-4) stated that on 06.02.2017 the Investigating Officer of the case handed over to him the dead bodies of the deceased for escorting them to the hospital and he also received the last worn clothes of both the deceased from the Medical Officers after the post mortem examinations of the dead bodies of the deceased. Jamil Ahmad, Patwari Halqa (PW-7) prepared the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh.PK). Shahzad Ahmad (PW-8) stated that on 01.04.2017 the appellant namely Wazeer got recovered his blood-stained clothes (P-12, P-13 and P-14) and a Toka stained with blood and on 02.04.2017 the appellant namely Muhammad Ali got recovered the pistol (P-16) having five live bullets (P-17/1- 5). Ibrar Hussain, ASI (PW-9) investigated the case on 06.02.2017 and detailed the facts of the investigation as conducted by him in his statement before the learned trial court. Iftikhar Ahmad 429/HC (PW-11) stated that on 06.02.2017 Amjad Hussain, SI (CW-2), the Investigating Officer of the case, handed over to him three sealed parcels said to contain blood stained earth and an empty recovered from the place of occurrence and on 15.02.2017 he handed over the said three sealed parcels to Ghulam Mohay ud Din, ASI (PW-5) for their onward transmission to the office of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore. Iftikhar Ahmad 429/HC (PW-11) further stated that on 01.04.2017 Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 6 Amjad Hussain, SI (CW-2), the Investigating Officer of the case, handed over to him two sealed parcels said to contain clothes and a Toka and on 02.04.2017 Amjad Hussain, SI (CW-2), the Investigating Officer of the case, handed over to him a sealed parcel said to contain a pistol which all the three sealed parcels he handed over to Amjad Hussain, SI (CW-2), the Investigating Officer of the case, for their onward transmission to the office of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore. 6.

Iftikhar Ahmad 429/HC (PW-11) stated that on 06.02.2017 Amjad Hussain, SI (CW-2), the Investigating Officer of the case, handed over to him three sealed parcels said to contain blood stained earth and an empty recovered from the place of occurrence and on 15.02.2017 he handed over the said three sealed parcels to Ghulam Mohay ud Din, ASI (PW-5) for their onward transmission to the office of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore. Iftikhar Ahmad 429/HC (PW-11) further stated that on 01.04.2017 Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 6 Amjad Hussain, SI (CW-2), the Investigating Officer of the case, handed over to him two sealed parcels said to contain clothes and a Toka and on 02.04.2017 Amjad Hussain, SI (CW-2), the Investigating Officer of the case, handed over to him a sealed parcel said to contain a pistol which all the three sealed parcels he handed over to Amjad Hussain, SI (CW-2), the Investigating Officer of the case, for their onward transmission to the office of the Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore. 6. The learned trial court also examined as many as two witnesses as court witnesses. Abbas Akhtar, DSP (CW-1) investigated the case on 26.05.2017 and detailed the facts of the investigation as conducted by him in his statement before the learned trial court. Amjad Hussain, SI (CW-2), the Investigating Officer of the case, investigated the case from 06.02.2017 till 26.05.2017, arrested the appellants namely Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad on 29.03.2017 and detailed the facts of the investigation as conducted by him in his statement before the learned trial court. 7. The complainant also got Dr. Tariq Aziz (PW-10) examined who on 06.02.2017 was posted as Senior Medical Officer at THQ hospital Khanpur and on the same day(actually being 07.02.2017at 12:00 a.m.) conducted the post mortem examination of the dead body of the deceased namely Muhammad Ehsan son of Muhammad Asghar. Dr. Tariq Aziz (PW-10), on examining the dead body of the deceased namely Muhammad Ehsan son of Muhammad Asghar, observed as under:- “DESCRIPTION OF INJURY (INCLUDING FINDING ON DISSECTION): 1. There was incised wound “W” shape 4cm x 6 cm looking made by sharp and heavy weapon. Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 7 2. There was incised pinna of left ear with incised neck from behind measuring about 12cm x 2.5cm. Wound cutting neck apart leaving only the point of neck area till hyoid bone region, also causing heavy bleeding and hemorrhage. 3. There is wound at mid epigastrium 1-2 cm away from midline left side, looking as an entry wound of fire arm.

Wound cutting neck apart leaving only the point of neck area till hyoid bone region, also causing heavy bleeding and hemorrhage. 3. There is wound at mid epigastrium 1-2 cm away from midline left side, looking as an entry wound of fire arm. There is an exit wound at back of chest 1cm away from midline as well while entering the abdominal cavity, it ruptured the liver and stomach organs causing bleeding and hemorrhage. 4. There is minor wound on right hand index finger measuring 1cm x 1cm x skin deep. 5. There are two minor tiny wound on right knee measuring 0cm x 0.4cm x skin deep. Injuries are simple........................ OPINION After conducting the autopsy, I was of the opinion that deceased had died of hemorrhage and shock due to injury No. 1, 2, 3, 4 & 5. Injuries No.1 & 2 caused by sharp weapon and injury No.3 caused by fire arm, injuries No. 4 & 5 caused by blunt. All these injuries are sufficient to cause death in ordinary course of nature. Expected duration between death and post (about 7-hours) and probable duration between injury and death (immediately)” The complainant also got Dr. Ayesha Anwar (PW-12) examined who on 06.02.2017 was posted as Woman Medical Officer at THQ hospital Khanpur and on the same day (actually being 07.02.2017at 12:00 a.m.) conducted the post mortem examination of the dead body of the deceased namely Tahira Bibi daughter of Ibrahim. Dr. Ayesha Anwar (PW-12) on examining the dead body of the deceased namely Tahira Bibi daughter of Ibrahim, observed as under:- “ DESCRIPTION OF INJURY (INCLUDING FINDING ON DISSECTION); 1. Neck and head are amputated at the level of shoulder, only tag of skin intact on right lateral side looking to be caused by sharpedge weapon. 2. Incised wound 3cm x 1cm skin deep on left parietal ear of head 8 cm away from left ear................ OPINION Expected duration between injury and death instantaneous and probable duration between death and postmortem within 24-hours..............

Incised wound 3cm x 1cm skin deep on left parietal ear of head 8 cm away from left ear................ OPINION Expected duration between injury and death instantaneous and probable duration between death and postmortem within 24-hours.............. Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 8 FINAL OPINION: After conducting the autopsy, I was of opinion that the deceased has died of the hemorrhage and shock due to injury No.1 & 2. These injuries were ante mortem and caused by sharp weapon. These injuries (especially injury No.1) was sufficient to cause death in ordinary course of nature.” 8. On 29.06.2019, the learned Deputy District Public Prosecutor gave up the witness namely Zafar Ullah as being unnecessary. On 13.07.2019, the learned Deputy District Public Prosecutor closed the prosecution evidence after tendering the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh.PT) regarding the analysis of the pistol recovered from the appellant namely Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad and the empty collected from the place of occurrence, the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh. PV) regarding the analysis of the blood-stained earth and the report of Punjab Forensic Science Agency, Lahore (Exh. PU) regarding the analysis of the Toka and clothes from the appellant namely Wazeer. 9. After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellants namely Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to question why this case against you and why the PWs have deposed against you, each of them replied that they had been involved in the case falsely and were innocent. The appellants namely Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad opted not to get themselves examined under section 340 Cr.P.C, however, produced a sworn affidavit (Mark-B) of Nazir Hussain as evidence in their defence.

After the closure of prosecution evidence, the learned trial court examined the appellants namely Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad under section 342 Cr.P.C. and in answer to question why this case against you and why the PWs have deposed against you, each of them replied that they had been involved in the case falsely and were innocent. The appellants namely Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad opted not to get themselves examined under section 340 Cr.P.C, however, produced a sworn affidavit (Mark-B) of Nazir Hussain as evidence in their defence. Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 9 10. On the conclusion of the trial, the learned Additional Sessions Judge/ Model Criminal Trial Court, Khanpur convicted and sentenced the appellants as referred to above. 11. The primary contention of the learned counsel for the appellants is that the whole case is fabricated and false. The learned counsel for the appellants argued that the prosecution remained unable to prove the facts in issue and did not produce any unimpeachable, admissible and relevant evidence to prove the same. The learned counsel for the appellants further contended that the statements of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6) were not worthy of any reliance. The learned counsel for the appellants also argued that the recoveries were full of procedural defects, of no legal worth and value and result of fake proceedings. The learned counsel for the appellants finally submitted that the prosecution has totally failed to prove the case against the appellants beyond the shadow of doubt. 12. On the other hand, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant/respondent No.2 contended that the prosecution has proved its case beyond the shadow of doubt by producing independent witnesses. They further argued that both the deceased died as a result of injuries suffered at the hands of the appellants. They further contended that the medical evidence also corroborated the statements of Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6).

They further argued that both the deceased died as a result of injuries suffered at the hands of the appellants. They further contended that the medical evidence also corroborated the statements of Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6). They further argued that the recovery of the pistol (P-16) from the appellant namely Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad and the recovery of the Toka from the appellant namely Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad also Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 10 corroborated the ocular account. They further contended that there was no occasion for the prosecution witnesses, who were related to the deceased, to substitute the real offenders with the innocent in this case. Lastly, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General and the learned counsel appearing on behalf of the complainant/respondent No.2 prayed for the rejection of appeals as lodged by the appellants namely Wazeer son of Nazir Ahmad and Muhammad Ali son of Mukhtiar Ahmad. 13. We have heard the learned counsel for the appellants, the learned Deputy Prosecutor General along with the learned counsel for the complainant and with their assistance perused the record and evidence recorded during the trial carefully. 14. According to the evidence produced by the prosecution during the course of the trial, the occurrence was witnessed by the three prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6). All the said three witnesses were not only related to each other but also with the deceased namely Muhammad Ehsan. The deceased Muhammad Ehsan was the real son of the prosecution witness namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), whereas the prosecution witness namely Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) admitted to being the cousin of Muhammad Asghar (PW-1) and the prosecution witness Muhammad Naeem (PW-6) admitted that Muhammad Asghar (PW-1) was his uncle.

All the said three witnesses were not only related to each other but also with the deceased namely Muhammad Ehsan. The deceased Muhammad Ehsan was the real son of the prosecution witness namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), whereas the prosecution witness namely Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) admitted to being the cousin of Muhammad Asghar (PW-1) and the prosecution witness Muhammad Naeem (PW-6) admitted that Muhammad Asghar (PW-1) was his uncle. It was further disclosed by the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6) that they were residents of the same area and their houses were constructed near to each other. Muhammad Asghar (PW-1) during cross-examination explained as under:- Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 11 “ Jalil Ahmad is my maternal cousin who is resident of Mad Bhoora........ Basti Mad Bhoora is at the distance of half kilometer from Adda Mad Bhoora and said Basti Mad Bhoora is on Eastern side of my house, Naeem s/o Akbar PW is my nephew. Naeem is also resident of Basti Mad Bhoora. The inter se distance between the houses of PW Naeem and PW Jalil is about 1/1 acres” Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) during cross-examination admitted as under:- “ Complainant is Muhammad Asghar is my cousin and BEHNOI......... Asghar is my cousin and BEHNOI and Ehsan deceased was my BHANJA........... PW Naeem is son of my cousin. Our Basti comprises of about 50 houses. My house is in middle of the Basti. My shop is at a distance of 9/10 acres from my Basti. The house of Asghar complainant is situated at Mad Bhoora Adda and my house is in the Basti.Inter se distance between my house and house of Naeem is 1 1⁄2 acre.” Muhammad Naeem (PW-6), during cross-examination admitted as under:- “ Asghar complainant is my uncle and PW Jalil is my relative.” It was also admitted by the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6) that the occurrence took place on a road that was crossing through the land of one

My shop is at a distance of 9/10 acres from my Basti. The house of Asghar complainant is situated at Mad Bhoora Adda and my house is in the Basti.Inter se distance between my house and house of Naeem is 1 1⁄2 acre.” Muhammad Naeem (PW-6), during cross-examination admitted as under:- “ Asghar complainant is my uncle and PW Jalil is my relative.” It was also admitted by the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6) that the occurrence took place on a road that was crossing through the land of one Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 12 Ghulam Rasool. Muhammad Asghar (PW-1) during cross-examination stated as under:- “ According to my information the place of occurrence and in the surrounding of the place of occurrence, there is land owned by Ghulam Rasool” During the course of cross-examination, it was also brought on record that the place of occurrence was at a distance of about one kilometre, a five minutes journey on a motorcycle, from the houses of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6). In this regard, Muhammad Asghar (PW-1) explained during cross- examination as under:- “ On 06.2.2017, my son Ehsan left the house at 5:40/5:41 PM on motorcycle for Chak 10/P to check the labour at brick-kiln. Inmates of the house were in the house and my son left the house when we were present on the back side of our house. About one minute later, I also left the house on motorcycle for Chak 10/P. I could not reach Chak 10/P as the occurrence took place in the way. When I reached at the place of occurrence, it was about 5:45.PM. The place of occurrence is situated in the area of Mauza Mad Bhoora Chak 10/P.......... Inter se distance between my house and place of occurrence is one kilometre”(emphasis supplied) We have also perused the scaled site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh. PK) as prepared by Jamil Ahmad, Patwari (PW-7) and the rough site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh. PQ) as prepared by Amjad Hussain, SI (CW-2), Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 13 the Investigating Officer of the case, and find that in both the said site plans (Exh.

PK) as prepared by Jamil Ahmad, Patwari (PW-7) and the rough site plan of the place of occurrence (Exh. PQ) as prepared by Amjad Hussain, SI (CW-2), Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 13 the Investigating Officer of the case, and find that in both the said site plans (Exh. PK and Exh. PQ) neither any house nor any place of business nor any place of work of any of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6) has been marked. As all the three prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6) were admittedly not the residents of any place near or around the place of occurrence and as the occurrence had taken place on a road lined on the sides by sugarcane crop at about Maghrib time, the presence of the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6), at the place of occurrence, could not be termed, as in the normal course of events, therefore, the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6) can be validly termed as “chance witnesses” and therefore were under a bounden duty to provide a convincing reason for their presence at the place of occurrence, at the time of occurrence and were also under a duty to prove their presence by producing some physical proof of the same. We have noted with grave concern that the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6) failed miserably to provide any consistent evidence as to the reason for their arrival at the place of occurrence and that too at the very moment when the same was taking place. According to prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6), the complainant of the case namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1) had a brickkiln constructed within the area of Chak No. 10/P and about 50-60 labourers were

We have noted with grave concern that the prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6) failed miserably to provide any consistent evidence as to the reason for their arrival at the place of occurrence and that too at the very moment when the same was taking place. According to prosecution witnesses namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1), Jalil Ahmad (PW-2) and Muhammad Naeem (PW-6), the complainant of the case namely Muhammad Asghar (PW-1) had a brickkiln constructed within the area of Chak No. 10/P and about 50-60 labourers were Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 14 also employed there, working at the said brickkiln. Section 103 of the Code of Criminal Procedure, 1898 is of vital significance to render search proceedings both transparent and creditable. The provisions of this section, unfortunately, are honoured more in disuse than compliance. To appreciate it better, this section is being reproduced:- "103.-- Before making a search. under this chapter, the officer or other person about to make it shall call upon two or more respectable inhabitants of the locality in which the place to be searched is situate to attend and witness the search and may issue an order in writing to them or any of them so to do. It is settled principle of law that for giving the benefit of the doubt it is not necessary that there should be so many circumstances rather if only a single circumstance creating reasonable doubt in the mind of a prudent person is available then such benefit is to be extended to an accused not as a matter of concession but as of right. The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of “Muhammad Mansha Vs. The State” (2018 SCMR 772) has enunciated the following principle: “Needless to mention that while giving the benefit of doubt to an accused it is not necessary that there should be many circumstances creating doubt. If there is a circumstance which creates reasonable doubt in a prudent mind about the guilt of the accused, then the accused would be entitled to the benefit of such doubt, not as a matter of grace and concession, but as a matter of right. It is based on the maxim, "it is better that ten guilty persons be acquitted rather than one innocent person be convicted". Reliance in this behalf can be made upon the cases of Tariq Pervez v. The State (1995 SCMR 1345), Ghulam Qadir and 2 others v. The State (2008 SCMR 1221), Muhammad Akramv.The State (2009 SCMR 230) and Muhammad Zamanv.The State (2014 SCMR 749).” Reliance is also placed on the judgment of the august Supreme Court of Pakistan Najaf Ali Shah Vs. the State (2021 S C M R 736) in which it has been observed in paragraph No.13 of page 236 as infra: “9. Mere heinousness of the offence if not proved to the hilt is not a ground to avail the majesty of the court to do complete justice. This is an established principle of law and equity that it is better that 100 guilty persons should let off but one innocent person should not suffer. As the preeminent English jurist William Blackstone wrote, "Better that ten guilty persons escape, than that one innocent suffer." Benjamin Franklin, who was one of the leading figures of early American history, went further arguing "it is better a hundred guilty persons should escape than one innocent person should suffer." All the contradictions noted by the learned High Court are sufficient to cast a shadow of doubt on the prosecution's case, which entitles the petitioner to the right of benefit of the doubt.

The said principle has been enunciated by the august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the judgment reported as Muhammad Inayat Versus The State ( 1998 SCMR 1854) wherein it has been held as under: “The Court would not interfere with acquittal merely because on re­appraisal of the evidence it comes to the conclusion different from that of the Court acquitting the accused provided both the conclusions are reasonably possible. If, however, the conclusion reached by that Court was such that no reasonable person would conceivably reach the same, and was impossible then this Court would interfere in exceptional cases on overwhelming proof resulting in conclusion and irresistible conclusion; and that too with a view only to avoid grave miscarriage of justice and for no other purpose. The important test visualised in these cases, in this behalf was that the finding sought to be interfered with, after scrutiny under the foregoing searching light, should be found wholly as artificial, shocking and ridiculous." The august Supreme Court of Pakistan in the case of “Mst. Sughran Begum and another Vs. Qaiser Pervaiz and others” (2015 SCMR 1142) has held as under: “On acquittal, an accused person earns twofold innocence particularly, in the case when there are concurrent findings to that effect by the trial Court and the Court of First Appeal (High Court), is the bedrock principle of justice. In a case of acquittal, the standard and principle of appreciation of evidence is entirely different from that in a case of conviction. Unless the concurrent findings of the two Courts below are found perverse, fanciful, arbitrary and are based on misreading and non-reading of material evidence causing miscarriage of justice, the Supreme Court would not lightly disturb the same because on Murder Reference No.17 of 2019 Criminal Appeal No. 489 of 2019 Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019. 44 reappraisal, another view might be possible therefore, sanctity is attached under the law to such concurrent findings in ordinary course.” Pursuant to the discussion made and conclusions arrived at above, the Petition for Special Leave to Appeal No.78 of 2019 as filed by the complainant, seeking permission to file an appeal against the acquittal of Jafar son of Mukhtiar Ahmad and Sabir Hussain son of Rab Nawaz (both since acquitted), is hereby dismissed. 25.
